title: Parasitic Injection Plan Entry
description: One agent-proposed splice of a parasitic subcircuit into the composer-generated testbench.cir. The composer reads a list of these entries and emits X-instances between the LISN/cable and the user fragment. The user's .cir is never mutated.

instance_name.description: SPICE instance refdes, must start with 'X_' (e.g. 'X_TRACE_VIN', 'X_VIA_RTN').

subckt_name.description: Which composer-generated subcircuit to instantiate. Only these three are supported in M2.10. The composer rejects unknown names.

nets.description: Ordered list of nets to wire to the subckt's ports. TRACE_RLC has 3 ports (IN OUT 0), VIA_L has 2 (IN OUT), CAP_ESR_ESL has 2 (IN OUT). The composer validates port count against subckt_name.

rationale.description: Human-readable engineering reason for the splice. E.g. 'series trace inductance between cable output and DUT input cap dominates the EMI loop'.

rule_id.description: Optional Rule_ID from the knowledge base that supports this splice. 'engineering_estimate' when no rule applies.

parasitic_id.description: Optional reference to the ParasiticEstimate id this injection uses (e.g. 'par-trace-L-iso-25x1'). Helps the composer pick the right .param prefix.

corner.description: Which corner of the parasitic band the X-instance should default to. The composer's .step sweep still walks all three corners; this field affects only the rendered X-instance's parameters when sweep is disabled.

agent.description: Optional agent name that proposed this injection. Defaults to 'parasitics' in M2.10.
